# MCP tools (auto-generated)

> This file is generated by `apps/mcp/scripts/dump-tools.ts`. Do not edit by hand.
> Run `pnpm --filter @keeta-agent-stack/mcp docs:tools` to regenerate.

**81 tools** across 9 modules. Conventions:

- `read` — does not change server, chain, or third-party state.
- `write` — mutates control-plane state, queues jobs, or hits external write endpoints.
- `signing` — invokes a signing path. Requires `KEETA_SIGNING_SEED` (worker-side) or `MCP_ALLOW_INLINE_SEEDS=true` (dev only).
- Field suffix `!` = required, `?` = optional. Defaults are not shown; consult the source.

## Bootstrap (seeds, accounts, faucet, network config)

<a id="bootstrap"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_generate_seed` | `read` | `{}` | Generate a new random cryptographic seed for Keeta account derivation. |
| `keeta_derive_account` | `read` | `{ seed!: string, index!: integer, algorithm?: enum("SECP256K1"|"SECP256R1"|"ED25519") }` | Derive a deterministic Keeta account from seed, index, and optional key algorithm. |
| `keeta_request_test_tokens` | `write` | `{ address!: string }` | Request free testnet KTA from faucet for a keeta_ address. |
| `keeta_get_network_config` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test") }` | Return network id, base token and network account for main/test. |
| `keeta_wallet_create_or_import` | `write` | `{ mode!: enum("create"|"import"), label?: string, address?: string, index?: integer, algorithm?: enum("SECP256K1"|"SECP256R1"|"ED25519"), register?: boolean, include_seed?: boolean }` | Create a new wallet (generate seed + derive address) or import an existing address. If API credentials are configured (OPS_API_KEY + API_URL/KEETA_AGENT_API_URL), registration uses /wallets/import. Seeds are never persisted by the API in this flow. |

## Discovery (introspect Keeta SDK)

<a id="discovery"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_list_sdk_methods` | `read` | `{ target!: string, network?: enum("main"|"test") }` | Discover available methods/properties on Keeta SDK objects. Targets: Client, UserClient, Builder, Account, Block, Permissions, Config, AnchorCatalog, AnchorService:<Name>, AnchorLib:<Name>. |

## Execute (dynamic Keeta SDK calls; seed-gated)

<a id="execute"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_client_execute` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), method!: string, args?: array }` | Execute any read-only method on Keeta Client. |
| `keeta_user_client_execute` | `signing` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), seed?: string, accountIndex?: integer, method!: string, args?: array }` | Execute a method on UserClient (authenticated or read-only with no seed). |
| `keeta_builder_execute` | `signing` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), seed?: string, accountIndex?: integer, operations!: array, autoPublish?: boolean }` | Execute a sequence of Builder operations and optionally publish blocks. Requires a signing seed: pass `seed` only when MCP_ALLOW_INLINE_SEEDS=true, otherwise the worker uses the server-pinned KEETA_SIGNING_SEED. |
| `keeta_anchor_execute` | `signing` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), seed?: string, accountIndex?: integer, subtarget!: enum("service"|"lib"|"metadata"), serviceName?: string, libModule?: string, method!: string, args?: array, rootAddress?: string }` | Execute anchor operations dynamically against service clients or lib modules. |

<a id="oracle"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_kta_rate` | `read` | `{ currency!: string, wallet_address?: string, _compact?: boolean }` | Live KTA rate by currency or ALL. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |

| `get_currencies_by_region` | `read` | `{ region?: string }` | List available currencies grouped by region. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `check_payment_status` | `read` | `{ transaction_id!: string, wallet_address?: string }` | Verify on-chain Keeta payment status. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_keeta_facts` | `read` | `{}` | Network facts and performance metrics. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_oracle_info` | `read` | `{ wallet_address?: string }` | Oracle identity, pricing, and capabilities. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_wallet_onboarding` | `read` | `{ platform?: string }` | Wallet onboarding flow for agents. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `activate_subscription` | `write` | `{ wallet_address!: string }` | Activate tier after sending KTA payment. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `validate_business_entity` | `read` | `{ entity_number!: string, country_code?: string, payment_amount?: number, payment_currency?: string, wallet_address?: string }` | Validate business registration details with AML context. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_compliance` | `read` | `{ region!: string, section?: string, wallet_address?: string }` | Compliance snapshot by region and section. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_kta_market_data` | `read` | `{ wallet_address?: string }` | Market cap/volume/change for KTA. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_sdk_snippet` | `read` | `{ operation!: string, network?: string, wallet_address?: string }` | Get production-ready Keeta SDK code snippets. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_anchor_info` | `read` | `{ service?: string, wallet_address?: string }` | Anchor ecosystem details (fx/kyc/asset movement/etc). (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_agent_onboarding` | `read` | `{ agent_type?: string }` | Autonomous agent onboarding flow. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `get_legal_rights` | `read` | `{ format?: string }` | Legal declaration and data rights statement. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `manage_social_alerts` | `write` | `{ action!: string, wallet_address!: string, platform?: string, frequency?: string, currency?: string, discordWebhook?: string, telegramBotToken?: string, telegramChatId?: string, slackWebhook?: string, apiKey?: string, apiSecret?: string, accessToken?: string, accessSecret?: string }` | Manage social alert subscriptions and delivery channels. (mirrored from KTA-Oracle). |
| `keeta_oracle_list_mirrored_tools` | `read` | `{}` | List all KTA-Oracle tools mirrored locally by this MCP server. |
| `keeta_oracle_discover_upstream_tools` | `read` | `{}` | Fetch current upstream KTA-Oracle MCP tool catalog (live discovery). |
| `keeta_oracle_call` | `read` | `{ name!: string, args?: object }` | Generic upstream Oracle tool call for forward compatibility with newly added tools. |
| `keeta_oracle_get_status` | `read` | `{}` | Fetch KTA-Oracle service status. |
| `keeta_oracle_list_tools` | `read` | `{}` | List public KTA-Oracle tools and tiers. |
| `keeta_oracle_get_rate` | `read` | `{ currency!: string, walletAddress?: string }` | Fetch live KTA exchange rate from KTA-Oracle for one currency. |
| `keeta_oracle_compare_rails` | `read` | `{ from?: enum("swift"|"bankwire"|"stripe"|"visa"|"all"), amount?: number, currency?: string }` | Compare Keeta vs legacy rails using KTA-Oracle pricing logic. |

## Oracle Payment Playbook (preview/execute)

<a id="oracle-payment"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `oracle.payment.preview` | `read` | `{ amount!: number, currency!: string, walletAddress?: string, recipientWallet?: string, compareFrom?: enum("swift"|"bankwire"|"stripe"|"visa"|"all"), complianceRegion?: string, network?: enum("main"|"test") }` | Preview a fiat → KTA payment. Use BEFORE oracle.payment.execute. Returns rates, rails, and execution instructions; never moves funds. Do NOT use for crypto-only swaps — call keeta_create_intent + keeta_quote_intent instead. |
| `oracle.payment.execute` | `write` | `{ correlationId!: string, intentId!: string (uuid), confirmation!: string }` | Execute a previously-previewed payment. IDEMPOTENT on correlationId — safe to retry on transient failures. Requires the operator to pass confirmation: "CONFIRM" exactly. Use ONLY after oracle.payment.preview has been shown to the user. |
| `oracle.subscription.list` | `read` | `{ walletAddress?: string, status?: enum("active"|"paused"|"all") }` | List the agent's recurring oracle payment subscriptions. Use for "what is the agent currently set up to send?" queries. Returns at most 200 rows; paginate by walletAddress if you need more. |
| `oracle.replay` | `write` | `{ correlationId!: string, reason!: string }` | Operator-gated replay of a previous payment run. REQUIRES OPS_API_KEY in the MCP server's environment. Use only for audit/forensics — never user-facing. |

## Agent Playbooks (high-level decision support)

<a id="agent-playbook"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_agent_payment_playbook` | `read` | `{ amount!: number, currency!: string, wallet_address?: string, recipient_wallet?: string, compare_from?: enum("swift"|"bankwire"|"stripe"|"visa"|"all"), compliance_region?: string, include_sdk_snippet?: boolean, network?: enum("main"|"test") }` | One-call payment planning for agents: rates, rail comparison, exchange instructions, optional compliance and SDK execution snippet. |
| `keeta_anchor_commercial_model` | `read` | `{ partner_size?: enum("small"|"medium"|"large"), withdrawal_delay_days?: enum("30"|"90") }` | Return the structured commercial and operational model for building a Keeta payment anchor. |

## Payment Anchors (CRUD + lifecycle)

<a id="anchors"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_list_payment_anchors` | `read` | `{}` | List payment anchors known to the Keeta Agent Stack API. |
| `keeta_get_payment_anchor` | `read` | `{ id!: string (uuid) }` | Get one payment anchor with current bond posture and recent events. |
| `keeta_create_payment_anchor_draft` | `write` | `{ adapter_id!: string, label!: string, corridor_key?: string, operator_ref?: string, supported_assets?: array, setup_fee_note?: string, volume_fee_bps?: number }` | Create a draft payment anchor record in the local Keeta Agent Stack API. |
| `keeta_update_payment_anchor_status` | `write` | `{ id!: string (uuid), status!: enum("draft"|"commercial_defined"|"bond_required"|"bond_pending_lock"|"active"|"withdrawal_requested"|"released"|"suspended") }` | Advance a payment anchor through its lifecycle using the local Keeta Agent Stack API. |
| `keeta_update_payment_anchor_bond` | `write` | `{ id!: string (uuid), amount_atomic!: string, asset_id?: string, delay_days!: enum(30|90), status!: enum("pending_lock"|"active"|"withdrawal_requested"|"released"|"failed_verification"), lock_tx_hash?: string, lock_account?: string, withdrawal_requested_at?: string (date-time), activated_at?: string (date-time), released_at?: string (date-time), verified?: boolean, verification_source?: enum("database"|"keeta"), verification_details?: object }` | Create or update the current bond record for a payment anchor in the local Keeta Agent Stack API. |
| `keeta_reconcile_payment_anchor_bonds` | `write` | `{ payment_anchor_id?: string (uuid), adapter_id?: string, reason?: string }` | Queue a bond reconciliation pass for all anchors or one specific anchor. |
| `keeta_run_payment_anchor_onboarding` | `write` | `{ payment_anchor_id?: string (uuid), adapter_id?: string, reason?: string, reconcile_bond?: boolean }` | Queue worker-driven onboarding progression for all anchors or one specific anchor. |

## Anchor Chaining (multi-hop graph queries)

<a id="anchor-chaining"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_describe_anchor` | `read` | `{}` | Describe the shape of the @keetanetwork/anchor module, including available services, lib modules, and AnchorChaining support. |
| `keeta_anchor_chaining_resolve_assets` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), from?: object, to?: unknown, max_step_count?: integer, only_allow_fx_like?: boolean, seed?: string, account_index?: integer }` | Resolve reachable source/target assets in the Keeta anchor-chaining graph, with rail inbound/outbound lists and pathLength distance (anchor 0.0.58's resolveAssets). |
| `keeta_anchor_chaining_list_assets` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), side?: enum("from"|"to"), location?: string, asset?: string, rail?: string, max_step_count?: integer, only_allow_fx_like?: boolean, seed?: string, account_index?: integer }` | List assets reachable from or to a side of the Keeta anchor-chaining graph (anchor 0.0.58 listAssets). |
| `keeta_anchor_chaining_graph_nodes` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), seed?: string, account_index?: integer }` | Compute all graph nodes (provider edges) in the Keeta anchor-chaining graph. |
| `keeta_anchor_chaining_find_paths` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test"), source!: object, destination!: object, seed?: string, account_index?: integer }` | Find candidate paths between a source and destination asset/location in the anchor-chaining graph. |
| `keeta_anchor_chaining_introspect` | `read` | `{ network!: enum("main"|"test") }` | Introspect the methods and properties of an AnchorChaining instance (for debugging upstream API drift). |

## Control Plane (Keeta Agent Stack API surface)

<a id="control-plane"></a>

| Tool | Class | Input | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `keeta_create_intent` | `write` | `{ strategyId?: string (uuid), walletId!: string (uuid), baseAsset!: string, quoteAsset!: unknown, side!: enum("buy"|"sell"), size!: string, maxSlippageBps!: integer, mode!: enum("simulate"|"live"), policyPackId?: string (uuid), venueAllowlist?: array, metadata?: object, validationContext?: object, additionalPrincipals?: array, requiresApproval?: boolean }` | Create a new execution intent in the control plane. Use this before quote, route, policy, and execute steps. |
| `keeta_quote_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Queue the quote-generation stage for an existing intent and return the job id and queue. |
| `keeta_route_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Queue the route-generation stage for an existing intent and return the job id and queue. |
| `keeta_evaluate_policy` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid), policyPackId?: string (uuid) }` | Queue the intent pipeline policy-evaluation stage (`POST /intents/:id/policy`). This is not the admin preview endpoint. |
| `keeta_execute_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Queue the execution stage for an existing intent and return the job id and queue. |
| `keeta_hold_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Place an intent on hold using the control-plane hold endpoint. |
| `keeta_release_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Release a previously held intent and allow the pipeline to resume from the next backend stage. |
| `keeta_approve_intent` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid) }` | Approve an intent that requires admin approval before execution. |
| `keeta_override_intent_route` | `write` | `{ intentId!: string (uuid), routePlanId!: string (uuid) }` | Override the selected route plan for an intent using an existing route plan id. |
| `keeta_list_policy_packs` | `read` | `{}` | List persisted policy packs that can be applied to admin policy previews or assigned to strategies. |
| `keeta_list_policy_rules` | `read` | `{}` | List the available policy rules and compositions known to the policy engine. |
| `keeta_preview_policy` | `read` | `{ intentId?: string (uuid), intent?: object, reason!: string, policyPackId?: string (uuid), configOverrides?: object, contextOverrides?: object }` | Run the admin policy preview endpoint. Use this to test an intent or policy pack without enqueuing the pipeline policy stage. |
| `keeta_create_policy_pack` | `write` | `{ name!: string, description?: unknown, rules?: array, compositions?: array }` | Create a persisted policy pack using existing rule ids and composition semantics. Use this to store reusable policy overlays. |
| `keeta_update_policy_pack` | `write` | `{ policyPackId!: string (uuid), name?: string, description?: unknown, rules?: array, compositions?: array }` | Update an existing persisted policy pack. Provide only the fields that should change. |
| `keeta_delete_policy_pack` | `write` | `{ policyPackId!: string (uuid) }` | Delete a persisted policy pack when it is no longer needed. |
| `keeta_get_strategy_policy_pack` | `read` | `{ strategyId!: string (uuid) }` | Read the explicit policy-pack assignment for a strategy. |
| `keeta_set_strategy_policy_pack` | `write` | `{ strategyId!: string (uuid), policyPackId!: string (uuid) }` | Assign a persisted policy pack to a strategy so future policy evaluation can resolve it by strategy. |
| `keeta_clear_strategy_policy_pack` | `write` | `{ strategyId!: string (uuid) }` | Remove an explicit strategy-level policy-pack assignment. |
| `keeta_get_wallet_balances` | `read` | `{ walletId!: string (uuid) }` | Fetch wallet balances from the control plane, including stored snapshots and Keeta network balances. |
| `keeta_get_chain_health` | `read` | `{}` | Read current Keeta chain health and network telemetry from the control plane. |
| `keeta_get_config_modes` | `read` | `{}` | Read current runtime modes such as live mode, network selection, and mock-adapter usage. |
| `keeta_get_strategy_templates` | `read` | `{}` | List strategy templates available to seed new strategies or inspect default configs. |
| `keeta_get_ops_metrics` | `read` | `{}` | Read recent operational metrics samples from the control plane. |
| `keeta_list_available_rails` | `read` | `{}` | List available rails and adapters with explicit production vs mock labeling. Non-production rails are marked with production=false. |
| `keeta_subscribe_webhook` | `write` | `{ targetUrl!: string (uri), eventTypes!: array, secret?: string, status?: enum("active"|"paused") }` | Create a webhook subscription for audit and anchor event delivery from the control plane. |
| `keeta_list_webhooks` | `read` | `{}` | List currently registered webhook subscriptions. |
| `keeta_list_events` | `read` | `{ after?: string (date-time), eventType?: string, intentId?: string (uuid), paymentAnchorId?: string (uuid), limit?: integer }` | List recent control-plane events with the same filters supported by the `/events` API route. |
| `keeta_stream_events` | `read` | `{ after?: string (date-time), eventType?: string, intentId?: string (uuid), paymentAnchorId?: string (uuid), limit?: integer, maxEvents?: integer, timeoutMs?: integer }` | Open the existing SSE event stream, collect events for a bounded window, and return the captured payloads as JSON. |
